questions.q: What is the name of the production house that filmmaker Yash Chopra founded in 1970?
questions.explanation: Yash Chopra founded Yash Raj Films in 1970, which grew into one of Hindi cinema's most powerful production houses.

questions.q: Filmmaker Yash Chopra is widely known by which nickname for his signature style of films?
questions.explanation: Yash Chopra was dubbed the 'King of Romance' for his lush love stories such as Silsila, Chandni and Veer-Zaara.

questions.q: In which year did the filmmaker Yash Chopra pass away?
questions.explanation: Yash Chopra died in 2012, shortly before the release of his final directorial film Jab Tak Hai Jaan.

questions.q: Which 1975 Amitabh Bachchan film about two estranged brothers was directed by Yash Chopra?
questions.explanation: Yash Chopra directed Deewaar (1975), starring Amitabh Bachchan and Shashi Kapoor as brothers on opposite sides of the law.

questions.q: The 1989 Yash Chopra romance starring Sridevi in the title role was called what?
questions.explanation: Chandni (1989), starring Sridevi, was a major Yash Chopra success that revived the romantic musical in Hindi cinema.

questions.q: Which actor played the lead role in Yash Chopra's final directorial film Jab Tak Hai Jaan (2012)?
questions.explanation: Jab Tak Hai Jaan (2012), Yash Chopra's last film as director, starred Shah Rukh Khan alongside Katrina Kaif and Anushka Sharma.

questions.q: Yash Chopra's son, who heads Yash Raj Films and directed Dilwale Dulhania Le Jayenge, is named what?
questions.explanation: Aditya Chopra, Yash Chopra's elder son, directed Dilwale Dulhania Le Jayenge (1995) and now heads Yash Raj Films.

questions.q: In which year was the filmmaker Yash Chopra born?
questions.explanation: Yash Chopra was born in 1932 in Lahore, in undivided British India.

questions.q: Which 1981 Yash Chopra film starred Amitabh Bachchan, Jaya Bhaduri and Rekha in a love triangle?
questions.explanation: Silsila (1981) featured Amitabh Bachchan, Jaya Bhaduri and Rekha, and is remembered for its emotionally charged love triangle.

questions.q: Shah Rukh Khan played a menacing, obsessive stalker in which 1993 Yash Chopra thriller?
questions.explanation: In Darr (1993), directed by Yash Chopra, Shah Rukh Khan played the obsessive stalker Rahul opposite Juhi Chawla and Sunny Deol.

questions.q: Yash Chopra began his directing career working under the banner of which elder brother, a noted filmmaker himself?
questions.explanation: Yash Chopra started out directing films for the B. R. Films banner of his elder brother B. R. Chopra before founding his own company.

questions.q: What was the title of Yash Chopra's 1959 directorial debut, made under B. R. Films?
questions.explanation: Dhool Ka Phool (1959), addressing illegitimacy and communal harmony, was Yash Chopra's directorial debut.

questions.q: Which 1965 Yash Chopra film is often cited as Hindi cinema's first true multistarrer, featuring Sunil Dutt, Raaj Kumar and others?
questions.explanation: Waqt (1965), with its lost-and-found ensemble plot and large star cast, is regarded as a pioneering Hindi multistarrer.

questions.q: The 1976 Yash Chopra film Kabhi Kabhie, known for its poetic title song, featured lyrics by which writer-poet?
questions.explanation: Sahir Ludhianvi wrote the lyrics for Kabhi Kabhie (1976), including the iconic title song.

questions.q: Which 2004 Yash Chopra film told a cross-border love story between an Indian air force officer and a Pakistani woman?
questions.explanation: Veer-Zaara (2004), starring Shah Rukh Khan and Preity Zinta, centered on an India-Pakistan love story.

questions.q: The taut 1969 suspense thriller Ittefaq, directed by Yash Chopra, was notable for having no what?
questions.explanation: Ittefaq (1969) was unusual for a Hindi film of its era in containing no songs, heightening its thriller tension.

questions.q: Yash Chopra's 1991 film Lamhe, praised for its bold theme, starred which actress in a double role?
questions.explanation: Lamhe (1991) starred Sridevi in a double role, playing both a mother and her daughter across two generations.

questions.q: Yash Chopra received India's highest cinema honour, the Dadasaheb Phalke Award, for which year?
questions.explanation: Yash Chopra was honoured with the Dadasaheb Phalke Award for 2001 for his contribution to Indian cinema.

questions.q: The duo of Shivkumar Sharma and Hariprasad Chaurasia scored several Yash Chopra romances including Silsila, Chandni, Lamhe and Darr. By what name was this composing pair known?
questions.explanation: The Shiv-Hari duo (santoor player Shivkumar Sharma and flautist Hariprasad Chaurasia) composed for several Yash Chopra films including Silsila, Chandni, Lamhe and Darr.

questions.q: Yash Chopra's 1997 musical Dil To Pagal Hai paired Shah Rukh Khan with which two leading actresses?
questions.explanation: Dil To Pagal Hai (1997) starred Shah Rukh Khan opposite Madhuri Dixit and Karisma Kapoor.

questions.q: In which city, then part of undivided India, was Yash Chopra born?
questions.explanation: Yash Chopra was born in Lahore in 1932, before the Partition of India.

questions.q: Yash Chopra frequently shot the songs of his romances in the picturesque valleys of which country?
questions.explanation: Yash Chopra became famous for filming lush song sequences amid the snow and meadows of Switzerland.

questions.q: Which 1973 Yash Chopra film starring Rajesh Khanna was the first feature produced under his own Yash Raj Films banner?
questions.explanation: Daag: A Poem of Love (1973), starring Rajesh Khanna, was Yash Chopra's first film produced and directed under his own Yash Raj Films banner.

questions.q: The 1961 Yash Chopra film Dharmputra, which dealt with communal tensions and Partition, gave which actor his first adult lead role?
questions.explanation: Dharmputra (1961) gave Shashi Kapoor his first lead role as an adult, and the film won the National Film Award for Best Feature Film in Hindi.

questions.q: Yash Chopra's 1984 film Mashaal featured a celebrated emotional street scene with Dilip Kumar crying for help. Who played the younger lead opposite him?
questions.explanation: In Mashaal (1984), Anil Kapoor played the young protagonist opposite the veteran Dilip Kumar.

questions.q: The soundtrack of Yash Chopra's Veer-Zaara (2004) was built from unused tunes left behind by the late composer Madan Mohan. Which legendary playback singer recorded several of its songs?
questions.explanation: The Veer-Zaara soundtrack used previously unrecorded Madan Mohan compositions, with Lata Mangeshkar singing several of its memorable songs.

questions.q: Yash Chopra's 1979 production Kaala Patthar, about coal miners, was inspired loosely by which real-life Indian disaster?
questions.explanation: Kaala Patthar (1979) drew inspiration from the 1975 Chasnala colliery mining disaster near Dhanbad, in which hundreds of miners died.

questions.q: The famous 'main aur meri tanhai' poetic monologue, voiced by Amitabh Bachchan, is associated with which Yash Chopra film?
questions.explanation: The 'main aur meri tanhai' lines, voiced by Amitabh Bachchan, feature in Yash Chopra's Silsila (1981).

questions.q: What was distinctive about the theatrical run of Dilwale Dulhania Le Jayenge, the 1995 Yash Raj Films production, at Mumbai's Maratha Mandir cinema?
options: It was the first Hindi film shown there
It was screened only on weekends
It ran continuously for over two decades
questions.explanation: Dilwale Dulhania Le Jayenge, produced by Yash Chopra's Yash Raj Films, ran continuously at Maratha Mandir for more than 20 years.

questions.q: Yash Chopra's 1965 multistarrer Waqt is credited with popularising which recurring plot device in Hindi cinema?
options: The lost-and-found family separated by fate
questions.explanation: Waqt (1965) popularised the 'lost-and-found' formula, in which a family is separated and reunited after years, later used in many Hindi films.
